skill.name: Burp Suite Web Application Testing
skill.description: Comprehensive guidance for using Burp Suite's core features for web application security testing including HTTP interception, request modification, vulnerability scanning, and manual testing workflows.
skill.summary: Learn to use Burp Suite for intercepting HTTP traffic, modifying requests, running vulnerability scans, and discovering web application security issues.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This skill is a documentation/tutorial file (SKILL.md) that teaches users how to use Burp Suite for authorized web application security testing. All static analysis findings are false positives: the detected 'external commands' are Markdown code formatting, 'network' references are localhost/Burp's local URLs, and 'path traversal' patterns are educational example payloads. No executable code or security risks present.

content.user_title: Master Web Application Security Testing with Burp Suite
content.value_statement: Web application security testing requires systematic methodology and the right tools. This skill provides comprehensive guidance for using Burp Suite to intercept traffic, modify requests, and discover vulnerabilities in authorized testing engagements.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through Burp Suite proxy configuration and HTTP traffic interception
Explain request modification techniques for testing business logic and access controls
Provide instructions for using Burp Repeater to manually test and replay requests
Walk through automated vulnerability scanning configuration and result analysis
Demonstrate Intruder attack setup for fuzzing and brute-force testing
Share common testing payloads for SQL injection, XSS, and path traversal vulnerabilities
limitations: This skill provides guidance only - Burp Suite software must be installed separately
Automated scanning features require Burp Suite Professional Edition
Testing should only be performed on applications you own or have explicit authorization to test
This skill does not execute any security tests - it provides educational documentation

use_cases.title: Security Engineer Performing Authorized Penetration Test
use_cases.description: A security professional needs to test a client's web application for vulnerabilities. They use this skill to configure Burp Suite proxy, intercept authentication requests, test for SQL injection in login forms, and run automated scans to identify security issues before providing remediation recommendations.

use_cases.title: Developer Learning Application Security
use_cases.description: A software developer wants to understand how attackers exploit web vulnerabilities. They follow this skill's guidance to set up Burp Suite, intercept their own application's traffic, modify parameters to test edge cases, and learn to identify and fix security issues in their code.

use_cases.title: Bug Bounty Researcher Testing Scope-Limited Targets
use_cases.description: A bug bounty hunter uses this skill to efficiently test in-scope web applications. They configure target scope to avoid out-of-scope requests, use Repeater to test interesting parameters, and apply Intruder attacks to discover authentication bypasses or business logic flaws.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner: Set Up Burp Suite Proxy
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me configure Burp Suite to intercept HTTP traffic from my browser. I have Burp Suite Community Edition installed. Walk me through launching the embedded browser, enabling interception, and verifying that requests are being captured in the HTTP history.

prompt_templates.title: Intermediate: Test for SQL Injection
prompt_templates.prompt: I found a login form with username and password fields. Guide me through sending the login request to Burp Repeater and testing for SQL injection vulnerabilities. Show me what payloads to try and how to analyze the responses for signs of SQL injection.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced: Configure Automated Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to run a comprehensive security scan on a web application at https://test-target.example.com. Help me configure a new scan in Burp Suite Professional with appropriate settings for a balanced scan, set the target scope correctly, and explain how to interpret the scan results.

prompt_templates.title: Expert: Business Logic Testing with Intruder
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm testing an e-commerce application for price manipulation vulnerabilities. Guide me through sending a cart POST request to Burp Intruder, setting up payload positions for the price parameter, configuring a list of test values, and analyzing responses to identify if the server trusts client-provided prices.

output_examples.input: User wants to intercept and modify a shopping cart request to test price manipulation
output: 1. Navigate to Proxy > Intercept and ensure 'Intercept is on'
2. Add item to cart in the browser - request will pause in Burp
3. Locate the price parameter in the intercepted POST request (e.g., price=9999)
4. Modify the value directly (e.g., change to price=1)
5. Click Forward to send the modified request
6. Complete checkout and verify if the manipulated price was accepted
7. Document finding: Server trusts client-provided price values - critical business logic flaw

output_examples.input: User wants to test a login form for SQL injection using Repeater
output: 1. Submit login credentials normally and capture the request in Proxy
2. Right-click the request and select 'Send to Repeater'
3. In Repeater tab, modify username to: admin' OR '1'='1'--
4. Click Send and analyze the response
5. Look for indicators: successful login, database errors, or different response length
6. If login succeeds with injection payload, document as SQL injection vulnerability
7. Test additional payloads to confirm and characterize the vulnerability

best_practices: Always define target scope before testing to prevent accidental out-of-scope requests and reduce noise in HTTP history
Use Burp's embedded browser for reliable proxy integration without manual certificate configuration
Save your Burp project regularly to preserve testing progress, site maps, and findings
Manually verify all automated scan results to eliminate false positives before reporting
Rate-limit automated scans and Intruder attacks to avoid triggering WAF blocks or causing denial of service
anti_patterns: Testing applications without explicit written authorization - only test systems you own or have permission to assess
Running aggressive scans against production systems without rate limiting - can cause service disruption
Ignoring target scope configuration - leads to capturing third-party requests and potential legal issues
Reporting automated scanner findings without manual verification - results in false positive reports

faq.question: What is the difference between Burp Suite Community and Professional editions?
faq.answer: Community Edition includes Proxy, Repeater, Intruder (rate-limited), and extensions. Professional adds automated vulnerability scanning, full Intruder functionality, and advanced tools. Community is free; Professional requires a paid license.

faq.question: How do I install the Burp CA certificate for HTTPS interception?
faq.answer: With Burp running, navigate to http://burp in your browser and click 'CA Certificate' to download. Install the certificate in your browser's or system's trusted certificate authorities. Restart your browser after installation.

faq.question: Why are my browser requests not appearing in Burp's HTTP history?
faq.answer: Verify that: (1) the proxy listener is active in Proxy > Options, (2) your browser proxy is set to 127.0.0.1:8080, (3) no firewall is blocking local connections, and (4) the target URL uses HTTP or you have the CA certificate installed for HTTPS.

faq.question: What does 'Send to Repeater' do and when should I use it?
faq.answer: Repeater allows you to manually modify and resend individual requests. Use it when you want to test specific parameter variations, fuzz inputs, or reproduce interesting behavior. It's ideal for targeted manual testing of specific endpoints.

faq.question: How can I test only specific parts of a web application?
faq.answer: Use Burp's target scope feature. Right-click the target host in Target > Site map and select 'Add to scope'. Then enable 'Show only in-scope items' in the display filter. This focuses your testing and prevents accidental out-of-scope requests.

faq.question: What should I do if Burp's automated scanner is running slowly?
faq.answer: Reduce scope to essential URLs, select a faster scan mode (Lightweight or Fast instead of Deep), increase Java heap size in Burp's startup settings, disable unused extensions, and close unnecessary Burp tabs to free resources.
